title: Guest Network Abuse Detection
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `TA-cisco_ios`, HPE Aruba CX syslog, firewall logs
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:guest" earliest=-24h
| stats dc(session_id) as concurrent_sessions, sum(bytes) as total_bytes by sponsor, guest_mac
| where concurrent_sessions>5 OR total_bytes>10737418240
| eval total_gb=round(total_bytes/1073741824,2)
| table sponsor, guest_mac, concurrent_sessions, total_gb
| sort -total_gb
description: Flags excessive concurrent guest sessions, high bandwidth, or repeated sponsor abuse — beyond simple guest usage volume (UC-17.1.4).
value: Security teams detect guest network abuse including bandwidth policy violations, excessive session durations, suspicious MAC address rotation suggesting attacker activity, and guests using the network as a pivot point for data exfiltration.
implementation: Map `bytes` from ISE or join firewall `src` for guest VLAN. Thresholds per org. Alert on sponsor accounts with many parallel guests.
visualization: Table (abuse candidates), Bar chart (bytes by sponsor), Single value (guests over threshold).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco ISE Guest Services and RADIUS accounting. Data in `index=nac` with `sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog`. Also, web proxy or firewall logs to correlate guest traffic with threat indicators. Key fields: `GuestUserName`, `CallingStationID`, `Acct-Input-Octets`, `Acct-Output-Octets`, `Acct-Session-Time`.
• Guest networks should have bandwidth limits, session duration caps, and content filtering. Abuse patterns: excessive bandwidth (torrenting), port scanning, connecting to known-bad IPs, excessive session duration, MAC spoofing.
• Create a `guest_bandwidth_policy.csv` lookup: `guest_type,max_daily_mb,max_session_hours,max_sessions_per_day`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify guest session data with accounting:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-24h
| where (IdentityGroup="GuestEndpoints" OR IdentityGroup="Guest") AND isnotnull(Acct-Session-Time)
| stats count by GuestUserName
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Bandwidth abuse:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-24h
| where (IdentityGroup="GuestEndpoints" OR IdentityGroup="Guest") AND Acct-Status-Type="Stop"
| eval mb_out=round(tonumber(coalesce('Acct-Output-Octets',0))/1048576, 1), session_hrs=round(tonumber(coalesce('Acct-Session-Time',0))/3600, 1)
| stats sum(mb_out) as total_mb_out max(session_hrs) as max_session_hrs count as sessions by GuestUserName, CallingStationID
| lookup guest_bandwidth_policy.csv guest_type as GuestUserName OUTPUT max_daily_mb max_session_hours
| eval bandwidth_exceeded=if(total_mb_out > tonumber(coalesce(max_daily_mb, 500)), "YES", "no"), duration_exceeded=if(max_session_hrs > tonumber(coalesce(max_session_hours, 8)), "YES", "no")
| where bandwidth_exceeded="YES" OR duration_exceeded="YES" OR sessions > 10
| sort - total_mb_out
```

Understanding this SPL: Flags guests who exceed bandwidth caps, session duration limits, or reconnect excessively. Excessive reconnections (>10 sessions/day) suggest automated activity or an attacker rotating sessions to evade detection. High outbound bandwidth from a guest could indicate data exfiltration or use of the guest network as a pivot point.

MAC rotation detection:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-24h
| where IdentityGroup="GuestEndpoints"
| stats dc(CallingStationID) as unique_macs by GuestUserName
| where unique_macs > 3
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Download a large file on the guest network and verify the bandwidth is captured.
(b) Verify thresholds are reasonable (not triggering on normal browsing).

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Guest bandwidth trend. Row 2 — Policy violations. Row 3 — MAC rotation suspects.
Alert: Immediate on bandwidth > 1 GB or MAC rotation > 5 in 24h.

Runbook:
1. **Bandwidth exceeded** — Throttle via ISE authorization profile or disconnect.
2. **MAC rotation** — Possible attacker. Block the guest account.
3. **Duration exceeded** — May be a contractor needing proper credentials.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No accounting data** — RADIUS accounting must be enabled on the NAD serving the guest VLAN.
• **Bandwidth always zero** — Switch may not support RADIUS accounting byte counters. Check platform capabilities.
grandmaExplanation: Our guest Wi-Fi has limits on how much data visitors can use and how long they can stay. We watch for people who go way over these limits or keep changing their device identity to avoid detection, because that could mean someone is misusing our network.
